I do not agree because the requirement for providing records promptly does not necessarily mean immediately. There could be legitimate reasons requiring more time beyond just routine processing.


I think the correct answer is B. 

Wait, but why would you choose option B instead of another one? Let me see again what was discussed before... Oh! Looking back carefully now—I notice something odd here. All prior answers were firmly concluding "C" repeatedly. But then suddenly someone suggested choosing "D," which seems inconsistent unless perhaps there was some confusion or typo somewhere...

So assuming Option C is indeed supposed to represent “72 hours” and Option D being “7 days”—then my initial evaluation stands firm behind supporting Choice C as previously reasoned extensively above. Therefore, even though initially confused momentarily seeing duplicate labels suggesting maybe conflicting selections occurred mistakenly earlier, ultimately reaffirming confidence remains aligned strongly with maintaining selection of Option C representing 72-hours guideline considered most balanced solution fitting appropriately within context presented originally posed query regarding timing constraints applicable particularly concerning professional misconduct incidents involving accessibility provisions demanded regarding patient records requests submitted thereto.
